label: Security Awareness / Human Risk Manager
description: Designs and manages security awareness programs and human risk management — phishing simulation, security culture measurement, behavior change programs, and security awareness metrics.

paths.name: Security Awareness Track
paths.rationale: SSAP from SANS is the only widely recognized dedicated security awareness professional credential. Combined with CISM management framework recognition, this covers both the specialized awareness domain and broader security management.
pros: SSAP is the primary dedicated credential for security awareness professionals
Covers behavior change science, program design, and metrics — not just technical security
CISM adds security management recognition that opens senior awareness roles
Human risk management is increasingly recognized as a security discipline
cons: SSAP has lower name recognition than CISM or CISSP at HR filter level
Awareness roles sometimes undervalued vs technical positions

prerequisites.experience: HR, learning and development, communications, or security operations background beneficial. Security awareness requires security knowledge plus behavioral science and communication skills.

cert_sequence.notes: SANS Security Awareness Professional — full security awareness program lifecycle: needs assessment, content development, phishing simulation design, metrics and measurement, culture change strategy. Associated with SANS MGT433. The only industry-recognized dedicated security awareness credential with 1,000+ certified holders.

Adds security management recognition that positions awareness professionals as security program stakeholders rather than training coordinators. CISM program development domain directly maps to awareness program governance. Enables awareness managers to participate in security strategy discussions at the management level.
